description_long: This is a modern urban traffic light mounted on a vertical black pole, featuring three circular signal lights arranged vertically at the top. Below the main signal, there is a smaller secondary signal light cluster, also vertically aligned. Attached to the pole is a rectangular pedestrian signal box, displaying illuminated red and green human figures for walk and don't walk indications. The housing and pole are constructed from matte-finished metal, with the signal lenses made of colored glass or plastic. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, suitable for installation at busy intersections. The structure is substantial, with a sturdy base and weather-resistant casing for outdoor use.
description: A black pole-mounted traffic light with three main vertical signal lights, a secondary signal cluster, and a pedestrian signal box displaying walk/don't walk icons.
description_view_0: Front view showing the three main green-lit signal lights and the lower secondary signal cluster.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the signal housings and the protruding pedestrian signal box.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back of the signal housings and the pole, with minimal visible detail.
description_view_3: Side view displaying the pedestrian signal box with illuminated red and green human figures, and the profile of the main signal lights.
